description: Figure 7 presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr (x = 0, 3, 6 wt%) steel strips, collected over a 2θ range of 30–100°. Subfigure (a) shows the CR20% cold-rolled condition, while subfigure (b) shows the CR20% + annealed condition, as stated in the caption. Each panel contains spectra for the 0Cr, 3Cr, and 6Cr alloys stacked vertically. All patterns exhibit reflections indexed to γ-austenite (FCC, red labels: (111)γ, (200)γ, (220)γ, (311)γ, (222)γ) and δ-ferrite/DO3 (BCC, blue labels: (110)δ, (200)δ, (211)δ, (220)DO3). With increasing Cr content, the relative intensity of the δ-ferrite/DO3 reflections increases, indicating a higher ferrite fraction. In the CR20% state (a), the 6Cr alloy displays a double peak between approximately 43.5° and 45.0° corresponding to separate (110)δ and (220)DO3 reflections. After annealing (b), this feature merges into a single broad (110)δ/(220)DO3 peak, suggesting that annealing promotes the transformation of δ-ferrite to the ordered DO3 phase. The figure confirms retention of the duplex γ + δ/DO3 microstructure after cold rolling and annealing.
